source_vendor: IETF MIB code components published on or after 2008-11-10
access_method: Fetch the authoritative RFC text and extract only the marked MIB or ASN.1 code component
rights_evidence: TLP section 4 licenses code components under the Revised BSD License; the IETF Trust FAQ explicitly includes MIBs and says the open license applies to IETF documents published on or after 2008-11-10. Preserve the BSD notice or permitted legend and record the source RFC.
permission_contact_action: No permission request for a qualifying module. Enforce per-RFC publication-date, stream, restrictive-legend, notice-retention, and attribution checks before activation.

source_vendor: IETF legacy MIB code components published before 2008-11-10
access_method: Fetch the authoritative RFC text; do not activate until the rights regime and notices for that exact RFC are reviewed
rights_evidence: The IETF Trust FAQ describes different rules for March 2005 to 2008-11-10 and still earlier material; the current BSD statement cannot safely be applied to every legacy MIB as a class.
permission_contact_action: Review each RFC and embedded module notice. Ask the IETF Administrative Director at iad@ietf.org when the applicable permission remains unclear.

source_vendor: IANA maintained MIB registries and directly linked MIB files
access_method: Fetch only protocol-registry data and MIB files directly linked from an IANA assignments page
rights_evidence: IANA and IETF dedicate applicable rights in Protocol Registries to CC0. Individual IANA MIB files can also carry an IETF Trust notice tied to an RFC; retain that notice and apply the corresponding code-component license.
permission_contact_action: No permission request for direct Protocol Registry data. Whitelist exact IANA assignment URLs and verify every raw MIB's embedded notice before release.

source_vendor: Cisco enterprise MIBs
access_method: Public Git clone or raw-file download; Cisco's SNMP FAQ identifies this repository as a retrieval route
rights_evidence: The public repository had no detected LICENSE (GitHub API license=null on 2026-07-13). Cisco's site terms grant only limited internal viewing and say Software Content requires its own specified license; reproduction or distribution is not granted by default.
permission_contact_action: Request written commercial redistribution permission from Cisco Legal covering all five scopes; until then use only non-public QA samples obtained consistently with the source terms.

source_vendor: Juniper Networks Junos enterprise MIBs
access_method: Download the release-specific ZIP or TAR package through the official SNMP MIB Explorer
rights_evidence: Juniper documents public download and compilation, but its legal notice says site content is protected and reproduction, modification, distribution, republication, display, or performance beyond resource use is prohibited.
permission_contact_action: Request a MIB-specific written license from Juniper Legal or the account team for indexing, rendered descriptions, API output, raw mirrors, and bulk export.

source_vendor: Arista Networks EOS proprietary MIBs
access_method: Download individual MIBs or the official all-MIB bundle from the public product-documentation page
rights_evidence: The source page offers proprietary MIB downloads. Arista's terms say no site information may be copied, reproduced, modified, published, uploaded, posted, transmitted, or distributed without prior written permission.
permission_contact_action: Ask Arista Legal for a written public/commercial redistribution grant. Do not infer a license from public download availability.

source_vendor: Aruba HPE AOS-CX and ArubaOS MIBs
access_method: Use the Aruba Service Portal and filter by product series and software release as directed by the official guide
rights_evidence: The guide directs users to the Service Portal and carries HPE copyright and commercial-software notices; it does not state a MIB-specific public redistribution license.
permission_contact_action: Obtain the actual archive and embedded notices through authorized access, then request an explicit MIB redistribution license from HPE Aruba if no separate license is present.

source_vendor: Fortinet FortiGate and Fortinet Core MIBs
access_method: Download from the FortiGate System SNMP page or the authenticated Fortinet support portal
rights_evidence: Fortinet authorizes copying only for non-commercial internal support of Fortinet products and prohibits public or commercial reproduction, distribution, publication, transmission, and derivative works without written permission.
permission_contact_action: Request written permission from Fortinet Legal for public commercial indexing and redistribution; keep downloaded files outside the public corpus meanwhile.

source_vendor: Palo Alto Networks enterprise SNMP MIBs
access_method: Download the enterprise MIB bundle from the official technical-documentation resource page
rights_evidence: Palo Alto Networks grants a personal non-commercial internal-use copy and prohibits modification, commercial/public use, transfer, or reproduction and distribution without prior written permission.
permission_contact_action: Request a separate written MIB content/API redistribution license through Palo Alto Networks Legal or the account team.

source_vendor: F5 BIG-IP enterprise MIBs
access_method: Copy from the BIG-IP appliance MIB directory or use the official product download route described by F5
rights_evidence: F5's terms grant a limited informational-use license and prohibit reproduction, modification, distribution, transmission, or republication unless expressly permitted in writing.
permission_contact_action: Request written permission from F5 Legal for the five scopes. A customer appliance copy may be used only in a local Tier P workflow unless its accompanying terms say more.

source_vendor: VMware vSphere NSX and vCenter MIBs distributed by Broadcom
access_method: Download the current ZIP attachment from the official KB or older packages from the authenticated Broadcom Support Portal
rights_evidence: The KB explicitly provides a current MIB ZIP and points to entitled support downloads, but no MIB-specific public redistribution license was visible in the reviewed primary pages.
permission_contact_action: Inspect the ZIP for an embedded license and request a written public redistribution grant from Broadcom/VMware Legal for any unresolved scope.

source_vendor: NetApp ONTAP custom MIB and traps.dat
access_method: Obtain current files from the NetApp Support Site or an authorized ONTAP node SPI web service
rights_evidence: NetApp's copyright notice limits documents to informational non-commercial use and expressly prohibits copying software to another server for further reproduction or redistribution; the ONTAP page gives access but no MIB-specific exception.
permission_contact_action: Request a MIB-specific public/commercial redistribution license from NetApp. Keep device-originated files local and unretained until granted.

source_vendor: Dell OpenManage and iDRAC MIBs
access_method: Download the public Dell OpenManage MIB ZIP from the official driver page and verify the published checksum
rights_evidence: Dell grants a limited personal non-commercial site-content license; its copyright page prohibits commercial/public display and mirroring without permission. No separate license is advertised on the download page.
permission_contact_action: Inspect the ZIP for controlling terms, then request written public/commercial redistribution permission from Dell Legal for unresolved scopes.

source_vendor: Synology DSM SRM and APM MIBs
access_method: Follow the official guide's MIB download link and import the complete mutually dependent set
rights_evidence: The official guide links the MIB set, while Synology's site terms reserve rights in site materials. No MIB-specific redistribution license was found on the reviewed guide or legal page.
permission_contact_action: Inspect every downloaded file for an embedded license and request written redistribution/API permission from Synology if absent.

source_vendor: MikroTik RouterOS MIB
access_method: Use the official RouterOS documentation link to the MikroTik tools download page
rights_evidence: MikroTik publishes a direct official download path and identifies MIKROTIK-MIB, but the reviewed source page does not state permission to republish, transform, expose by API, or bulk export it.
permission_contact_action: Inspect the raw MIB header and obtain a written five-scope license from MikroTik before any public activation.

source_vendor: Schneider Electric APC PowerNet MIB
access_method: Download the versioned powernet MIB directly from Schneider Electric's public document page
rights_evidence: Schneider's terms state site content is proprietary, other use is prohibited, systematic extraction is forbidden, and no copyright transfer or grant is implied.
permission_contact_action: Request explicit written permission from Schneider Electric/APC for public commercial indexing, rendered content, API output, mirroring, and bulk export.

source_vendor: Eaton Network-M3 UPS ATS and sensor MIBs
access_method: Use the authenticated device web UI Supported MIBs button documented in the official user guide
rights_evidence: Eaton limits site materials to personal non-commercial download and expressly prohibits software reproduction or redistribution outside its accompanying license; no MIB-specific public license was found.
permission_contact_action: Obtain the archive under authorized access, inspect accompanying terms, and request written commercial redistribution permission from Eaton Legal.

source_vendor: Vertiv Geist rack power MIBs
access_method: Use the product web UI ZIP action documented by Vertiv to download MIB and CSV files
rights_evidence: Vertiv permits document use only for informational non-commercial or personal use and says documents may not be copied or posted on a network computer or broadcast; no MIB-specific exception was found.
permission_contact_action: Request written MIB-specific commercial redistribution and API permission from Vertiv Legal; otherwise support only local Tier P processing.

source_vendor: Huawei enterprise network product MIBs
access_method: Use the official support site product and release filters to download the matching MIB_XXXX.zip package
rights_evidence: Huawei states that software rights are retained and site content may not be reproduced, modified, promoted, distributed, mirrored, or stored in an information-retrieval system without prior written consent.
permission_contact_action: Request explicit written permission from Huawei Legal for each scope and for automated update access; do not crawl the support portal.

source_vendor: Nokia SR OS and VSR MIBs
access_method: Obtain the entitled VSR or vSIM release ZIP from the Nokia Support Portal; the package includes a MIBs directory
rights_evidence: Nokia documents MIBs as a component of an entitled software package. The reviewed EULA describes use in support of internal business operations and no public MIB redistribution grant was identified.
permission_contact_action: Request a separate written MIB redistribution/API license from Nokia Legal or the account team before extracting package content into the public service.

source_vendor: Extreme Networks EOS EXOS and related MIBs
access_method: Download public EOS TAR or ZIP bundles; EXOS and several other families require Extreme Portal login and release selection
rights_evidence: Extreme documents public EOS downloads and authenticated EXOS downloads, but its legal page and MIB source page do not provide a clear MIB-specific public redistribution grant.
permission_contact_action: Review the Terms of Use PDF and archive-level notices with Extreme Legal; request one written license covering both public and portal-delivered families.

source_vendor: QNAP QES and QNE NAS MIBs
access_method: Use the authenticated NAS Control Panel SNMP page and click Download to obtain NAS.mib
rights_evidence: QNAP documents device-local MIB download. Its software terms are non-distributable, but the reviewed pages do not establish whether NAS.mib has a separate license; no public redistribution grant was found.
permission_contact_action: Inspect NAS.mib notices on an authorized device and ask QNAP Legal for explicit rights before any server-side retention or public output.
